mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trajectory in the federal funds rate increases the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically reducing present values and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of FEDFUNDS level with respect to 20-day forward returns is positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0% signals deteriorating household income expectations and weakening aggregate demand, triggering corporate earnings revisions that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 5.0%, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in retail sales and industrial production captures a coordinated contraction in both final demand and goods supply, amplifying recession risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both decrease over a 1-month window
failure_condition: The joint z-score combination of RSAFS and INDPRO does not produce a negative Rank IC exceeding 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, elevated industrial production growth indicates supply-side overheating that prompts further rate hikes, tightening financial conditions and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, rising INDPRO coincides with positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of weeks over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts reflect strengthening residential investment and construction employment, driving cyclical earnings growth that generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of month-over-month HOUST changes with 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected acceleration in consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns following CPIAUCSL monthly changes above 0.4% are not significantly negative compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in payroll employment and a declining unemployment rate signals a tight labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE does not achieve a positive Rank IC greater than 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals broad-based aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate sales and profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Following quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 0.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.
